campaignName: Launch Reel 3 — A Video Goes In
objective: Condensed full product-journey Reel (upload through progress tracking) for tool-curious viewers who haven't seen the whole product, not just one mechanics observation.
hook: A pitching video goes in. This is what comes back.
coreInsight: One slow-motion upload becomes a six-phase breakdown, one clear priority, personalized drills, and a trackable plan — the full journey, not one flagged pattern.
openLoop: The hook promises to show the entire input-to-output journey — the viewer watches to see the whole thing, not just a single screen.
payoff: Each product stage appears in sequence, ending on the progress tracker, proving the full journey is real and complete.

scenes.visualBeat: Pattern-interrupt hook — opens on the product itself
scenes.cameraDirection: Real screen recording: athlete selecting and uploading a slow-motion clip in the app, hard cut in.
scenes.soundDesign: Single low tick on the cut in.

scenes.cameraDirection: Screen recording of the processing/progress state, sped up 3-4x so the wait reads as motion, not a pause.
scenes.voiceOver: One upload. Real processing — not a guess.
scenes.soundDesign: Processing-hum SFX, sped up in pitch slightly with the visual speed-up.

scenes.cameraDirection: Screen recording scrolling through the six phase screenshots quickly, each phase label visible for roughly half a second.
scenes.soundDesign: Quick UI tap accent per phase.

scenes.visualBeat: Proof it's built from the real video
scenes.cameraDirection: Cut to the real full-speed pitch footage that was uploaded, matched to one of the phase screenshots just shown.

assetReferences.description: The real uploaded pitch, matched to a phase screenshot

scenes.cameraDirection: Screen recording showing the single flagged priority card, highlighted in electric blue.
scenes.voiceOver: One clear priority — not a wall of numbers.

scenes.cameraDirection: Screen recording scrolling the drill list, quick 1-second cut to real drill footage matching the named drill.
bRoll: One real drill rep matching the named drill
scenes.soundDesign: UI tap accent, then clean cut to drill footage.

scenes.cameraDirection: Screen recording of the rolling training calendar view.
scenes.voiceOver: And a plan you can actually track.
scenes.soundDesign: UI tap accent, music builds slightly.

scenes.cameraDirection: Static end card: wordmark small at top, CTA centered, price beneath.
scenes.voiceOver: Start your Pitch Nav analysis. Twenty-five dollars a month.

voiceOverScript: One upload. Real processing — not a guess. Six phases, broken down automatically. Built from your actual video. One clear priority — not a wall of numbers. Personalized drills that match it. And a plan you can actually track. Start your Pitch Nav analysis. Twenty-five dollars a month.
bRollInstructions: Every screen must be a real, live screen recording of the actual product — no mocked-up UI. The real-footage cutaway in Scene 4 must actually correspond to the phase screenshots shown in Scene 3, not a generic clip.
editingNotes: This Reel opens on product UI instead of pitching footage, unlike the other four launch Reels — that's intentional; it's the product-demonstration format and the UI itself is the hook payoff. Frame changes every 1-3 seconds throughout. Price appears once, on the final card only.
soundDesign: Confident, propulsive, tech-forward music bed that builds steadily from Scene 2 through the end card. UI tap accents on every screen transition, kept short and consistent so they read as a system sound, not a gimmick.

assetReferences.description: Full journey: upload, processing, six phases, priority, drills, progress calendar
The uploaded pitch + one matching drill demonstration
